---
title: "Day 15 — Backprop ninja: every backward pass by hand"
subtitle: "Friday, 7 August 2026 · Week 3 · Tokens and attention · Study day · 3-4 h"
---

::: {.day-nav .kind-study}
[← Day 14](day-14.qmd) · [All 50 days](../timeline.qmd) · [Day 16 →](day-16.qmd)
:::

::: {.why}
You have called loss.backward() for two weeks without ever computing what it computes. Today you write every backward pass yourself - cross-entropy, the output matmul, tanh, BatchNorm, the input matmul, the embedding gather - and check each gradient against PyTorch's. This is the hardest day of the bootcamp so far. Budget the frustration, not just the minutes.
:::

## What you should be able to do tonight

- For y = x @ W + b you can write dx, dW and db from dy alone, including which axis db sums over and why that sum exists at all.
- You can derive dlogits = softmax(logits); dlogits[range(n), Yb] -= 1; dlogits /= n, and say why it is the same observed-minus-fitted gradient as multinomial logistic regression.
- You can point at the 1/(n-1) in bnvar and say where Bessel's correction shows up again in the backward pass.

## Tasks <span class='task-total'>185 min</span>

::: {.task-list}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [20 min]{.task-min} Rerun the forward-pass cells in the makemore_part4_backprop.ipynb you set up last night, then read the cmp() helper line by line until you can say exactly what ex, app and maxdiff each test and why all three get reported instead of just one.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [85 min]{.task-min} Watch to roughly 1:05 and do exercise 1 the slow way: pause at every new tensor, write your own dlogprobs, dprobs, dcounts_sum_inv, dcounts, dlogits, dh, dW2, db2, dhpreact, dbngain, dbnraw, dbnvar_inv, dbnvar, dbndiff2, dbndiff, dhprebn, dbnmeani, dembcat, dW1, db1, demb, dC before he reveals each one. Every cmp() should print exact: True.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [55 min]{.task-min} Watch the rest: exercise 2 collapses the whole cross-entropy backward into three lines, exercise 3 does the same for BatchNorm, and exercise 4 trains the MLP with your manual backward and no loss.backward() at all.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [25 min]{.task-min} Write two rules in your notes in your own words - how a gradient flows back through broadcasting (you sum over the axis that was broadcast), and where the transpose goes in a matmul backward. If either refuses to stick, D2L 5.3 draws the same thing as a computational graph.
:::
:::

## Resources

- [Karpathy L5 - makemore part 4, Becoming a Backprop Ninja (1h55m)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q8SA3rM6ckI) [video]{.res-kind}
- [nn-zero-to-hero repo - lectures/makemore/makemore_part4_backprop.ipynb](https://github.com/karpathy/nn-zero-to-hero) [repo]{.res-kind}
- [D2L 5.3 - Forward Propagation, Backward Propagation, and Computational Graphs](https://d2l.ai/chapter_multilayer-perceptrons/backprop.html) [textbook]{.res-kind}

## Deliverable

::: {.deliverable}
makemore_part4_backprop.ipynb with every cmp() in exercises 1-3 printing exact: True (or approximate: True with maxdiff under 1e-8), plus half a page of notes holding the broadcasting rule and the matmul-transpose rule.
:::

## Checkpoint

::: {.checkpoint}
hpreact = embcat @ W1 + b1, with embcat (32, 30), W1 (30, 200), b1 (200,). Given dhpreact of shape (32, 200), write dembcat, dW1 and db1 - shapes and where each transpose goes - and say which axis db1 sums over and why the sum is there.
:::

## Prepare for tomorrow

Ten minutes tonight so Day 16, Saturday, 8 August 2026 starts clean. This is what the evening email sends you.

::: {.task-list .prep}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Download SLP3 Ch6 (6.pdf) so you are not waiting on it tomorrow.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Open lectures/makemore/makemore_part5_cnn1.ipynb and run the data-loading cell to confirm names.txt is where the notebook expects it.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Run python -c "import torch.nn.modules.module as m; print(m.__file__)" and note the path - you will read that file tomorrow.
:::
:::
